summary.text: User has denied the permission.
User has granted the permission.
User hasn't granted or denied the permission yet.
A subscription object that allows to conveniently remove an event listener from the emitter.

Removes an event listener for which the subscription has been created.
After calling this function, the listener will no longer receive any events from the emitter.
Timestamp (in ms since the Unix epoch) associated with the pedometer event.
Type of the pedometer event, indicating whether updates have paused or resumed.
Number of steps taken between the given dates.
Callback function providing event result as an argument.
Permission expiration time. Currently, all permissions are granted permanently.
An object obtained by permissions get and request functions.

Indicates if user can be asked again for specific permission.
If not, one should be directed to the Settings app
in order to enable/disable the permission.
Determines time when the permission expires.
A convenience boolean that indicates if the permission is granted.
Determines the status of the permission.
Checks user's permissions for accessing pedometer.

Get the step count between two dates.

As [Apple documentation states](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coremotion/cmpedometer/1613946-querypedometerdatafromdate?language=objc):
> Only the past seven days worth of data is stored and available for you to retrieve. Specifying
> a start date that is more than seven days in the past returns only the available data.

As [Android's documentation states](https://developer.android.com/health-and-fitness/recording-api):
> **Note:**
`LocalRecordingClient`

stores up to 10 days of data.
> Data is only accessible while there is an active subscription.

On Android, call
`subscribeRecordingAsync()`

before querying step history and keep it active if you want
the previous days to be available.

Returns whether the pedometer is enabled on the device.

, indicating whether the pedometer is

summary.text: Check whether pedometer pause/resume event tracking is supported on this device.

Check whether step history is supported on this device.
On iOS, historical data is collected automatically (up to seven days).

> **Note:** On Android, this checks whether the required Play Services Recording API components are available.
> Step history is only accessible while there is an active
`subscribeRecordingAsync()`
subscription.

, indicating whether
historical step count data is available on this device.

summary.text: Asks the user to grant permissions for accessing pedometer.
Start pedometer pause/resume event tracking.
Stop pedometer pause/resume event tracking.

Subscribe to pedometer tracking. Step count will be tracked by Google Play Services
Recording API, if available, until unsubscribed. Subsequent calls are safe and ignored.

As [Android's documentation states](https://developer.android.com/health-and-fitness/recording-api):
> **Note:**
`LocalRecordingClient`

stores up to 10 days of data.
> Data is only accessible while there is an active subscription.

content.text: a promise that fulfills when the subscription is successful.

summary.text:
Unsubscribe from pedometer tracking.

As [Android's documentation states](https://developer.android.com/health-and-fitness/recording-api):
> **Note:** To free up resources, you should make sure to unsubscribe from
> the collection of sensor data when your app is no longer in need of it.
> Unsubscribing will also reset the historical data that was collected.

content.text: a promise that fulfills when the unsubscription is successful.

summary.text:
Listen for pedometer pause/resume events emitted by the underlying platform.
Call [
`startEventUpdatesAsync`

](#starteventupdatesasync) to begin receiving events.

> Event delivery is best-effort and generally only while the app is running.
> Do not rely on it while the app is in the background or terminated.
> On Android, events are derived from walking/running activity transitions.

Subscribe to pedometer updates.

> Pedometer updates will not be delivered while the app is in the background.
The
`getStepCountAsync`

method can be used to get the step count between two dates.
On Android, historical step counts require Play Services Recording API support and an active
`subscribeRecordingAsync()`
subscription.

content.text: when you would like to unsubscribe the listener.

summary.text:
A callback that is invoked when new step count data is available. The callback is
provided with a single argument that is [
`PedometerResult`
](#pedometerresult).
